name: Dojigiri Yasutsuna
description: Dojigiri Yasutsuna is one of the Five Great Swords of Japan (天下五剣, Tenka Goken), renowned for its exceptional craftsmanship and legendary sharpness. Forged by the swordsmith Yasutsuna of Hoki Province during the Heian period (794-1185), its name, meaning 'Oni Cutter', derives from its purported use in slaying the infamous oni (demon) Shuten-doji. The sword is a tachi, a type of long sword typically worn slung from the belt with the cutting edge down. Its blade is noted for its elegant curvature and intricate hamon (temper line), a testament to Yasutsuna's skill. Beyond its physical attributes, Dojigiri Yasutsuna embodies the samurai spirit of courage, skill, and duty. It is considered a national treasure of Japan and is currently housed in the Tokyo National Museum, serving as a tangible link to Japan's rich mythological and historical past. Its association with Shuten-doji cemented its place in folklore, representing the triumph of good over evil and the power of human ingenuity to overcome supernatural threats. The sword is not merely a weapon but a symbol of power, craftsmanship, and the enduring legacy of Japanese sword-making tradition. Possessing or wielding Dojigiri Yasutsuna in legends is often associated with immense strength and the ability to vanquish even the most formidable foes. The sword's legend continues to inspire awe and respect, representing the pinnacle of Japanese sword-making and the enduring power of myth.
subtitle: The Oni Cutter, Sword of Legends
origin: Forged by Yasutsuna of Hoki Province during the Heian period, specifically to slay the oni Shuten-doji.

keyMyths.title: Slaying of Shuten-doji
keyMyths.description: Minamoto no Yorimitsu and his retainers used Dojigiri Yasutsuna to behead the oni Shuten-doji, who terrorized the region around Mount Oe. The sword's sharpness was crucial in overcoming the demon's supernatural resilience.

keyMyths.title: The Five Great Swords
keyMyths.description: Dojigiri Yasutsuna is one of the Tenka Goken, the Five Great Swords of Japan, each renowned for its quality and historical significance. These swords represent the pinnacle of Japanese sword-making craftsmanship.

sources.author: Kanzan Sato
sources.description: Provides detailed information on the history and craftsmanship of Japanese swords.
sources.text: Provides detailed information on the history and craftsmanship of Japanese swords.
